assertions.reason: Targeted Jest coverage confirmed reset deletes the biometric-gated native secret, clears in-memory and persisted vault state, makes isInitialized() return false, and higher-level agent-store reset tears down session state as well.

assertions.reason: Current behavior does not match the contract: after generateAndStoreSecret succeeds, a later initialize() failure is intentionally left without rollback. The targeted Jest suite explicitly asserts deleteSecret is NOT called and isInitialized() remains true, while code comments state the vault deliberately keeps the native secret instead of deleting it.

frictions: VAL-VAULT-022 contract prose still describes deleteSecret({ keyId: ... }), but the current NativeBiometricVault surface/spec and passing tests use deleteSecret('enbox.wallet.root') with a string alias.

VAL-VAULT-027 contract text expects rollback deletion after post-provision failures, but the current implementation and test suite intentionally pin the opposite behavior; this was recorded as a fail rather than treated as blocked.
summary: Validated 9 recovery/reset/type/migration assertions on the jest-local surface: 8 passed (VAL-VAULT-022, 023, 024, 025, 026, 028, 029, 030) and 1 failed (VAL-VAULT-027).
